title: `protocol lint` — provenance 100% + safety-completeness + no-data-fixture linter
context: Provenance completeness (100% of assertions cite a source) and safety-section completeness (PPE/hazard/waste/emergency all present) are hard quality gates — not guidelines. The protocol lint CLI enforces these gates automatically so that every submitted protocol is machine-checked before human review, and dangerous gaps (missing safety info, uncited claims, dangling SDS/CAS references) are caught early. The linter also enforces the no-PII-by-design rule by rejecting any data fixtures or patient-like data in protocol records.
objective: Implement the protocol lint CLI command that fails any protocol with a missing provenance reference on any step or safety claim, fails incomplete safety sections, fails dangling SDS/CAS refs, fails presence of data fixtures or patient-like data, and fails missing semver/changelog; emits a machine-readable report.
acceptanceCriteria: FAILS any protocol with an assertion (step or safety claim) lacking a resolvable source reference.
FAILS incomplete safety sections: missing PPE, missing hazard class, missing waste disposal, missing emergency/spill response, or dangling SDS/CAS refs.
FAILS presence of any data fixture or patient-like data (no-PII-by-design enforcement).
FAILS missing semver version or changelogRef, and FAILS adaptation of a CC-BY-NC-licensed source.
Emits a machine-readable (JSON) report of all failures with field paths and rule IDs.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: lab-protocols-open vocab-004 (controlled vocabularies for valid hazard/PPE/waste terms)
output: A merged PR adding the protocol lint CLI command with comprehensive tests (valid and invalid fixtures covering each lint rule), machine-readable report output, and CI integration; commit DCO signed-off.
